name: Money Mizer Pawns and Jewelers of Jacksonville, FL (Blanding Blvd)
description_short: Money Mizer operates two Jacksonville pawn shop locations offering collateral-based loans, jewelry sales, and firearm transactions as a licensed FFL dealer.
description_long:
Money Mizer Pawns and Jewelers is a multi-state pawn shop chain with two locations in Jacksonville, Florida: one on Acme Street (32211) and one on Blanding Boulevard (32244). The company has been operating across the Southeast with additional locations in Alabama, Georgia, and Panama City, suggesting established operational infrastructure and experience in the pawn lending industry.

The company offers traditional pawn shop services including collateral-based loans (with renewal/extension options), jewelry sales, and general merchandise trading. They operate as a licensed Federal Firearms License (FFL) dealer on GunBroker, indicating they buy, sell, and facilitate firearms transactions. The company maintains online sales channels through eBay stores and provides online payment options for customers to renew loans or pay on layaway accounts.

Money Mizer distinguishes itself through multi-channel accessibility: customers can conduct business in-person at either Jacksonville location, online via eBay, through GunBroker for firearms, and via online payment portals. The company advertises upcoming "new & improved payment system" improvements and provides multiple contact methods (phone, fax, email) for both locations. Their FFL licensing and GunBroker presence indicate compliance with federal firearms regulations.

As a pawn shop, Money Mizer is best suited for consumers needing quick cash against collateral (jewelry, electronics, firearms) who prefer in-person transactions or have items to sell/pawn. The main caveat is that pawn loans typically carry higher interest rates than traditional personal loans, and customers risk losing collateral if loans aren't repaid. The company provides no transparency on their website regarding interest rates, loan terms, or fee structures—critical information for informed borrowing decisions.
pros: Two convenient Jacksonville locations for in-person service
Licensed FFL dealer enabling firearms transactions on GunBroker
Online payment system for loan renewals and layaway payments
Multiple sales channels including eBay store for liquidating inventory
Multi-state presence (8+ locations across AL, GA, FL) suggests operational stability
Established business infrastructure with fax, email, and phone support
Upcoming payment system improvements indicate customer service investment
cons: Website provides no disclosure of interest rates, fees, or loan terms
No information about loan duration, renewal policies, or redemption periods
No customer reviews, testimonials, or third-party ratings available on website
Limited accessibility information (hours of operation, accessibility accommodations not listed)
No transparent explanation of how collateral values are assessed or determined
best_for: Consumers needing immediate cash who own jewelry, electronics, or firearms to pledge as collateral
Individuals seeking to buy firearms or jewelry through a licensed dealer with online shopping convenience
People in Jacksonville preferring in-person transactions over online-only lenders
diagnosis: Money Mizer is best for Jacksonville-area consumers with collateral to pledge who need fast cash or want to buy jewelry/firearms in person. The critical caveat is the complete absence of rate and term transparency on their website—borrowers should contact them directly before committing to understand the full cost of pawn loans.
services: In-person retail transactions at two locations
Firearms appraisal services (implicit via FFL licensing)
